title: What is CTI / CITS?

detail.title: What is CTI / CITS?
detail.short_info: CTI / CITS Admission 2026 online form is open at cits2026.nimi.co.in, and the last date to apply has been extended to 17 May 2026. Check eligibility, important dates, application fees, AICET exam pattern, syllabus, counselling process, and official notification details from nimionlineadmission. in.

content.text: Craft Instructor Training Scheme (CITS) is a one-year training programme aimed at preparing skilled instructors for Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and other vocational training centres across India. Candidates can take admission in various trades offered at National Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs), Institutes of Training of Trainers (IToTs), and affiliated government as well as private institutes through the online admission process.

The CITS Admission 2026 application last date has now been extended to 17 May 2026, giving aspirants additional time to complete the registration process. The admission process includes online registration, All India Common Entrance Test (AICET), result declaration, counselling, and final seat allotment. Candidates can check important dates, eligibility criteria, application steps, exam pattern, counselling details, and official links in one place.

rows: Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, Government of India
Online application + AICET entrance exam + counselling
Engineering & non‑engineering trades in NSTI / IToT / Government & Private institutes

content.text: You can later plug in exact “start date, last date, exam date” from the official notification as soon as it is released.
CITS full form and purpose

CITS stands for Craft Instructor Training Scheme. It is a specialised training programme for those who want to become instructors in ITIs and similar training institutions. CTI is commonly used in admission notices and candidate searches for this same admission process.

The scheme focuses on upgrading both technical knowledge and teaching skills. Trainees learn trade theory at instructor level, modern teaching methods, lesson planning, evaluation, and how to handle practical training in workshops.
Why CITS is important for ITI candidates

For ITI, Diploma and Degree holders, CITS is a strong pathway to build a career in teaching and training. Many state and central level recruitment rules either require or strongly prefer CITS‑qualified candidates for Instructor posts. Having CITS on your profile often makes you more competitive in ITI Instructor recruitments and related skill‑training jobs.
You should keep this section as a table and update it with official dates once you have them:

rows: Last date to submit the online form extended

content.text: In your live article, clearly mention that dates are as per official notification and advise users to double‑check from the official website before applying.
Educational qualification
Typically, CITS eligibility is based on a relevant technical qualification. You can structure it like this:

items: Candidate should have one of the following in the relevant trade/discipline: National Trade Certificate (NTC) National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) Diploma in relevant engineering / non‑engineering discipline Degree in relevant engineering / non‑engineering discipline
National Trade Certificate (NTC)
National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC)
Diploma in relevant engineering / non‑engineering discipline
Degree in relevant engineering / non‑engineering discipline

The qualification must be in a trade or branch that is mapped to the CITS trade applied for. For example, for CITS in Electrician‑related trade, a candidate might need ITI / Diploma / Degree in Electrical or related discipline, as per the trade‑mapping list in the prospectus.

content.text: Make it clear that detailed trade‑wise eligibility is given in the official CITS prospectus on nimionlineadmission.in, and candidates must check that before choosing their trade.
Age limit
You can add a generic, safe note:

items: In earlier sessions, CITS did not prescribe a strict upper age limit, but candidates must have completed the qualifying course before joining the programme.
Any age relaxations or special conditions (for SC / ST / OBC / EWS / PwD etc.) will be clearly mentioned in the official notification and should be followed strictly.

content.text: Instead of guessing amounts, define the structure:

items: Candidates have to pay an entrance exam fee while submitting the online application form.
The fee is usually different for categories such as General / OBC / EWS and SC / ST / PwD / Women.
Payment is made online through debit card, credit card, net banking or UPI on the admission portal.

content.text: Candidates are advised to refer to the official CITS prospectus for detailed trade-wise syllabus and exam pattern information.
In your article, encourage users to check the current year’s CITS prospectus or notification for the exact fee amount and category‑wise details.

sections.heading: How to Apply Online for CTI / CITS Admission 2026

content.text: This is a key transactional section; write it step‑wise for maximum clarity.
Official websites and links

items: Official CITS admission website: Click Here
CTI / CITS 2026 online registration link: Click Here

content.text: Step‑by‑step CTI / CITS online form process

items: Open the official portal Visit nimionlineadmission.in in your browser. On the homepage, look for “CITS Admission 2026-27” or “Apply Online” and click on it. Alternatively, go directly to cits2026.nimi.co.in/register.php if that link is active for the current session.

Register as a new candidate Click on “New Registration” and enter your basic details such as name, mobile number, email ID and password. Complete OTP verification if required and create your login account.
Login to your dashboard Use your registered email/mobile and password to log in. After login, you will see the CITS 2026 application form.
Fill personal details Enter your name, parents’ names, date of birth, gender, category (General / OBC / SC / ST / EWS), address and ID proof details exactly as per your official documents.
Enter educational details Fill in the details of your ITI / NTC / NAC / Diploma / Degree - trade or branch, year of passing, board or university, marks/percentage or grade, and certificate numbers.
Select CITS trade and exam city From the list, choose the CITS trade you want to apply for. Then select your preferred AICET exam city or centre from the available options.

Upload documents Upload scanned copies of your passport‑size photograph, signature and supporting documents (educational certificates, caste certificate, disability certificate, etc.) in the prescribed size and file format.
Pay application fee Proceed to the payment section and pay the required examination fee online. Once payment is successful, note down the transaction ID or download the payment receipt.

Submit and print confirmation Review all the details entered in the form carefully. After verifying, click on “Final Submit”. Download the filled application form and confirmation page and take a printout for future reference.

content.text: The selection process usually follows these broad steps:

items: Online application Candidates submit their forms and pay the entrance exam fee within the specified date.
AICET (All India Common Entrance Test) All registered candidates appear for the CITS entrance exam at their allotted centre / city.
Merit list and result Based on AICET scores, a merit list is prepared. Cut‑off marks may vary by category and trade.
Counselling and choice filling Qualified candidates participate in online counselling, where they choose their preferred NSTI / IToT / other institutions and trades.

Seat allotment and reporting Seats are allotted as per score, category, preferences and availability. Candidates must report to the allotted institute with original documents and pay admission fees to confirm their seat.

sections.heading: Important Points for CTI / CITS Aspirants

content.text: To add value beyond pure information, include a short guidance section:

items: Check trade mapping carefully Ensure that your ITI / Diploma / Degree trade is eligible for the CITS trade you are selecting. An incorrect selection can lead to rejection even after clearing the exam.
Do not rely only on unofficial dates Always verify application dates, exam dates and any changes from nimionlineadmission.in, as schedules can be updated.
Prepare systematically for AICET Focus on your ITI trade syllabus, practise previous year questions if available, and strengthen reasoning and basic maths.
Keep documents ready Keep scanned copies of photo, signature, certificates and category proofs in the required format before starting the online form.

content.text: You can create a small navigational list:

items: CITS Online Admission Website (official): Click Here
CTI / CITS 2026 Apply Online: Click Here
